name: pygerrit2

metadata.name: pygerrit2
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Client library for interacting with Gerrit's REST API

documentation:
# Pygerrit2 - Client library for interacting with Gerrit Code Review's REST API

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pygerrit2.svg
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/pygerrit2.svg
```

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/dpursehouse/pygerrit2.svg?branch=master
```

```{image} https://api.dependabot.com/badges/status?host=github&repo=dpursehouse/pygerrit2
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
```

Pygerrit2 provides a simple interface for clients to interact with
[Gerrit Code Review] via the REST API.

## Copyright and License

Copyright 2011 Sony Ericsson Mobile Communications. All rights reserved.

Copyright 2012 Sony Mobile Communications. All rights reserved.

Copyright 2016 David Pursehouse. All rights reserved.

Licensed under The MIT License. Please refer to the [LICENSE] file for full
license details.

[gerrit code review]: https://gerritcodereview.com/
[license]: https://github.com/dpursehouse/pygerrit2/blob/master/LICENSE

functions.name: pygerrit2.from_json
documentation:
Extract values from JSON data.

:arg dict json_data: The JSON data
:arg str key: Key to get data for.

:Returns: The value of `key` from `json_data`, or None if `json_data`
    does not contain `key`.

functions.name: pygerrit2.escape_string
documentation:
Escape a string for use in Gerrit commands.

:arg str string: The string to escape.

:returns: The string with necessary escapes and surrounding double quotes
    so that it can be passed to any of the Gerrit commands that require
    double-quoted strings.

classes.name: pygerrit2.GerritReviewMessageFormatter

methods.name: pygerrit2.GerritReviewMessageFormatter.__init__
documentation: See class docstring.

methods.name: pygerrit2.GerritReviewMessageFormatter.append
documentation:
Append the given `data` to the output.

:arg data: If a list, it is formatted as a bullet list with each
    entry in the list being a separate bullet.  Otherwise if it is a
    string, the string is added as a paragraph.

:raises: ValueError if `data` is not a list or a string.

methods.name: pygerrit2.GerritReviewMessageFormatter.is_empty
documentation:
Check if the formatter is empty.

:Returns: True if empty, i.e. no paragraphs have been added.

methods.name: pygerrit2.GerritReviewMessageFormatter.format
documentation:
Format the message parts to a string.

:Returns: A string of all the message parts separated into paragraphs,
    with header and footer paragraphs if they were specified in the
    constructor.

Helper class to format review messages that are sent to Gerrit.

:arg str header: (optional) If specified, will be prepended as the first
    paragraph of the output message.
:arg str footer: (optional) If specified, will be appended as the last
    paragraph of the output message.

documentation: Create a Gerrit container for the given version and return an API.

classes.name: livetests.GerritContainer

methods.name: livetests.GerritContainer.__init__
documentation: Construct a GerritContainer with the given version.
Gerrit container.

classes.name: livetests.TestGerritAgainstLiveServer

methods.name: livetests.TestGerritAgainstLiveServer.test_put_with_json_dict
documentation:
Test a PUT request passing data as a dict to `json`.

Tests that the PUT request works as expected when data is passed
via the `json` argument as a `dict`.

Creates the test project which is used by subsequent tests.

methods.name: livetests.TestGerritAgainstLiveServer.test_put_with_data_dict
documentation:
Test a PUT request passing data as a dict to `data`.

Tests that the PUT request works as expected when data is passed
via the `data` argument as a `dict`.

methods.name: livetests.TestGerritAgainstLiveServer.test_post_with_data_dict_and_no_data
documentation:
Test a POST request passing data as a dict to `data`.

Tests that the POST request works as expected when data is passed
via the `data` argument as a `dict`.

methods.name: livetests.TestGerritAgainstLiveServer.test_post_with_json_dict
documentation:
Test a POST request passing data as a dict to `json`.

Tests that the POST request works as expected when data is passed
via the `json` argument as a `dict`.

Creates the change which is used by subsequent tests.

methods.name: livetests.TestGerritAgainstLiveServer.test_put_with_data_as_string
documentation:
Test a PUT request passing data as a string to `data`.

Tests that the PUT request works as expected when data is passed
via the `data` parameter as a string.

Creates a change edit that is checked in the subsequent test.

methods.name: livetests.TestGerritAgainstLiveServer.test_put_json_content
documentation: Test a PUT request with a json file content (issue #54).

methods.name: livetests.TestGerritAgainstLiveServer.test_get_base64_data
documentation:
Test a GET request on an API that returns base64 encoded response.

Tests that the headers can be overridden on the GET call, resulting
in the data being returned as text/plain, and that the content of the
response can be base64 decoded.

methods.name: livetests.TestGerritAgainstLiveServer.test_get_patch_zip
documentation: Test a GET request to get a patch file (issue #19).

methods.name: livetests.TestGerritAgainstLiveServer.test_put_with_no_content
documentation: Test a PUT request with no content.

methods.name: livetests.TestGerritAgainstLiveServer.test_review
documentation: Test that a review can be posted by the review API.
Run tests against a live server.

methods.name: unittests.TestNetrcAuth.test_basic_auth_from_netrc
documentation: Test that the HTTP basic auth is taken from netrc.

documentation: Test that the HTTP digest auth is taken from netrc.

methods.name: unittests.TestNetrcAuth.test_basic_auth_from_netrc_fails
documentation: Test that an exception is raised when credentials are not found.

methods.name: unittests.TestNetrcAuth.test_digest_auth_from_netrc_fails
documentation: Test that an exception is raised when credentials are not found.

methods.name: unittests.TestNetrcAuth.test_default_to_basic_auth_from_netrc
documentation: Test auth defaults to HTTP basic from netrc when not specified.

methods.name: unittests.TestNetrcAuth.test_default_to_no_auth_when_not_in_netrc
documentation: Test auth defaults to none when not specified and not in netrc.

documentation: Test explicit anonymous access when credentials are in netrc.

Test that netrc authentication works.

classes.name: unittests.TestKwargsTranslation

methods.name: unittests.TestKwargsTranslation.test_data_and_json
documentation: Test that `json` and `data` cannot be used at the same time.

methods.name: unittests.TestKwargsTranslation.test_data_as_dict_converts_to_json_and_header_added
documentation:
Test that `data` dict is converted to `json`.

Also test that a Content-Type header is added.

methods.name: unittests.TestKwargsTranslation.test_json_is_unchanged_and_header_added
documentation: Test that `json` is unchanged and a Content-Type header is added.

methods.name: unittests.TestKwargsTranslation.test_json_no_side_effect_on_subsequent_call
documentation:
Test that subsequent call is not polluted with results of previous.

If the translate_kwargs method is called, resulting in the content-type
header being added, the header should not also be added on a subsequent
call that does not need it.

methods.name: unittests.TestKwargsTranslation.test_kwargs_unchanged_when_no_data_or_json
documentation: Test that `json` or `data` are not added when not passed.

methods.name: unittests.TestKwargsTranslation.test_data_as_string_is_unchanged
documentation: Test that `data` is unchanged when passed as a string.
Test that kwargs translation works.

functions.name: pygerrit2.rest._decode_response
documentation:
Strip off Gerrit's magic prefix and decode a response.

:returns:
    Decoded JSON content as a dict, or raw text if content could not be
    decoded as JSON.

:raises:
    requests.HTTPError if the response contains an HTTP error status code.

classes.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritRestAPI

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritRestAPI.__init__
documentation: See class docstring.

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritRestAPI.make_url
documentation:
Make the full url for the endpoint.

:arg str endpoint: The endpoint.

:returns:
    The full url.

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritRestAPI.translate_kwargs
documentation: Translate kwargs replacing `data` with `json` if necessary.

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritRestAPI.get
documentation:
Send HTTP GET to the endpoint.

:arg str endpoint: The endpoint to send to.
:arg bool return_response: If true will also return the response

:returns:
    JSON decoded result.

:raises:
    requests.RequestException on timeout or connection error.

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritRestAPI.put
documentation:
Send HTTP PUT to the endpoint.

:arg str endpoint: The endpoint to send to.

:returns:
    JSON decoded result.

:raises:
    requests.RequestException on timeout or connection error.

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritRestAPI.post
documentation:
Send HTTP POST to the endpoint.

:arg str endpoint: The endpoint to send to.

:returns:
    JSON decoded result.

:raises:
    requests.RequestException on timeout or connection error.

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritRestAPI.delete
documentation:
Send HTTP DELETE to the endpoint.

:arg str endpoint: The endpoint to send to.

:returns:
    JSON decoded result.

:raises:
    requests.RequestException on timeout or connection error.

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritRestAPI.review
documentation:
Submit a review.

```
arg str change_id

The change ID.

arg str revision

The revision.

arg str review

The review details as a .

returns

JSON decoded result.

raises

requests.RequestException on timeout or connection error.
```

Interface to the Gerrit REST API.

:arg str url: The full URL to the server, including the `http(s)://`
    prefix. If `auth` is given, `url` will be automatically adjusted to
    include Gerrit's authentication suffix.
:arg auth: (optional) Authentication handler.  Must be derived from
    `requests.auth.AuthBase`.
:arg boolean verify: (optional) Set to False to disable verification of
    SSL certificates.
:arg requests.adapters.BaseAdapter adapter: (optional) Custom connection
    adapter. See
    https://requests.readthedocs.io/en/master/api/#requests.adapters.BaseAdapter

classes.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritReview

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritReview.__init__
documentation: See class docstring.

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritReview.set_message
documentation:
Set review cover message.

:arg str message: Cover message.

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritReview.set_tag
documentation:
Set review tag.

:arg str tag: Review tag.

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritReview.add_labels
documentation:
Add labels.

```
arg dict labels

Labels to add, for example
```

Usage:

```
add_labels({'Verified': 1,
            'Code-Review': -1})
```

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritReview.add_comments
documentation:
Add inline comments.

```
arg dict comments

Comments to add.
```

Usage:

```
add_comments([{'filename': 'Makefile',
               'line': 10,
               'message': 'inline message'}])

add_comments([{'filename': 'Makefile',
               'range': {'start_line': 0,
                         'start_character': 1,
                         'end_line': 0,
                         'end_character': 5},
               'message': 'inline message'}])
```

methods.name: pygerrit2.rest.GerritReview.__str__
documentation: Return a string representation.

Encapsulation of a Gerrit review.

:arg str message: (optional) Cover message.
:arg dict labels: (optional) Review labels.
:arg dict comments: (optional) Inline comments.
:arg str tag: (optional) Review tag.
